title: भैरव कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री भैरव कवचम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
ॐ अस्य श्री भैरवकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। श्री भैरवो देवता।
ह्रीं बीजम्। क्लीं शक्तिः। श्रीं कीलकम्।
सर्वापद्विनाशनार्थे, रक्षार्थे च जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
कल्पान्तभैरवं देवं नीलाञ्जनसमप्रभम्।
त्रिनेत्रं दशहस्तं च शूलखड्गधरं विभुम्॥
नागयज्ञोपवीतं च नागकुण्डलभूषितम्।
वामाङ्कस्थितदेवीकं ध्यायेत्कालभयापहम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे भैरवः पातु ललाटे वटुको विभुः।
भ्रुवौ मे रक्षतां भद्रो नेत्रे रुद्रोऽवतात् सदा॥ १॥

कर्णौ मे रक्षतां क्रोधो नासिकां घ्राणभैरवः।
मुखं मे रक्षतां कण्ठं जिह्वां पातु महेश्वरः॥ २॥

दन्तान् मे रक्षतां दंष्ट्री ओष्ठौ रक्षतु भैरवः।
चिबुकं रक्षतां नित्यं कण्ठं पातु कपालभृत्॥ ३॥

स्कन्धौ मे रक्षतां शूली बाहू मे दण्डपाणिकः।
करौ मे रक्षतां खड्गी अङ्गुलीः पातु शङ्करः॥ ४॥

नखान् मे रक्षतां रौद्रो हृदयं पातु वामनः।
स्तनौ मे रक्षतां कालो नाभिं पातु गणाधिपः॥ ५॥

कटिं मे रक्षतां मर्त्यो ऊरू मे पातु भीषणः।
जानुनी रक्षतां रक्तो जङ्घे मे पातु भूतपः॥ ६॥

गुल्फौ मे रक्षतां श्रेष्ठः पादौ रक्षतु भैरवः।
सर्वाङ्गं रक्षतां नित्यं सर्वव्यापी महेश्वरः॥ ७॥

पूर्वस्यां रक्षतां भद्रो दक्षिणे कपिलो विभुः।
पश्चिमे रक्षतां भीमो वायव्ये सर्वतोमुखः॥ ८॥

उत्तरे रक्षतां कालो ऐशान्यां सर्वतः शिवः।
ऊर्ध्वं पातु शिखी देवो ह्यधस्ताद्वटुकोऽवतु॥ ९॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
कवचं भैरवस्येदं यः पठेत् प्रयतः सदा।
सर्वापद्विनिर्मुक्तः सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नुयात्॥

राजद्वारे श्मशाने च सङ्ग्रामे शत्रुसङ्कटे।
भूतप्रेतपिशाचेभ्यो न भयं विद्यते क्वचित्॥

॥ इति श्री भैरव कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Bhairav Kavacham ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Om Asya Shri Bhairavakavacha-stotra-mantrasya Brahma Rishiph.
Gayatri Chhandah. Shri Bhairavo Devata.
Hrim Bijam. Klim Shaktih. Shrim Kilakam.
Sarva-apad-vinashanaarthe, Raksharthe Cha Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Kalpanta-bhairavam Devam Nilanjana-samaprabham.
Trinetram Dashahastam Cha Shula-khadga-dharam Vibhum.
Naga-yajnopavitam Cha Naga-kundala-bhushitam.
Vamanka-sthita-devikam Dhyayet-kala-bhayaapaham.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Bhairavah Patu Lalate Vatuko Vibhuh.
Bhruvau Me Rakshatam Bhadro Netre Rudro-avataat Sada. || 1 ||

Karnau Me Rakshatam Krodho Nasikam Ghrana-bhairavah.
Mukham Me Rakshatam Kantham Jihvam Patu Maheshvarah. || 2 ||

Dantan Me Rakshatam Damshtri Oshthau Rakshatu Bhairavah.
Chibukam Rakshatam Nityam Kantham Patu Kapalabhrit. || 3 ||

Skandhau Me Rakshatam Shuli Bahu Me Dandapaanikah.
Karau Me Rakshatam Khadgi Angulih Patu Shankarah. || 4 ||

Nakhan Me Rakshatam Raudro Hridayam Patu Vamanah.
Stanau Me Rakshatam Kalo Nabhim Patu Ganadhipah. || 5 ||

Katim Me Rakshatam Martyo Uru Me Patu Bhishanah.
Januni Rakshatam Rakto Janghe Me Patu Bhutapah. || 6 ||

Gulphau Me Rakshatam Shreshthah Padau Rakshatu Bhairavah.
Sarvangam Rakshatam Nityam Sarva-vyapi Maheshvarah. || 7 ||

Purvasyam Rakshatam Bhadro Dakshine Kapilo Vibhuh.
Pashchime Rakshatam Bhimo Vayavye Sarvato-mukhah. || 8 ||

Uttare Rakshatam Kalo Aishanyam Sarvatah Shivah.
Urdhvam Patu Shikhi Devo Hy-adhastad-vatuko-avatu. || 9 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Kavacham Bhairavaasyedam Yah Pathet Prayatah Sada.
Sarva-apad-vinirmuktah Sarva-siddhim-avaapnuyaat.

Rajadvare Shmashane Cha Sangrame Shatru-sankate.
Bhuta-preta-pishachebhyo Na Bhayam Vidyate Kvachit.

|| Iti Shri Bhairav Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री भैरव कवच में भगवान भैरव से शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग और सभी दिशाओं की रक्षा की प्रार्थना है।

ध्यान में कल्पान्त भैरव का स्वरूप वर्णित है जो नीलाञ्जन के समान कान्ति वाले, त्रिनेत्र, दस हाथों वाले, त्रिशूल और खड्ग धारण करने वाले, नागयज्ञोपवीत और नागकुण्डल से भूषित हैं।

कवच में मस्तक से पैर तक और सभी आठ दिशाओं में भैरव के विभिन्न रूपों से रक्षा की याचना है। जो इस कवच का नित्य पाठ करता है, वह सर्वापदाओं से मुक्त होता है, राजदरबार, श्मशान, युद्ध और शत्रु-संकट में भूत-प्रेत-पिशाच से उसे कभी भय नहीं होता।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीभैरवकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्रीभैरवो देवता। ह्रीं बीजम्। क्लीं शक्तिः। श्रीं कीलकम्। श्रीभैरवप्रीत्यर्थे भैरवकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish a divine protective armor around the devotee through bhairav's grace
Shields every part of the body from head to toe with Bhairav's protection
Guards against evil spirits, black magic, and occult attacks
Traditionally recited for removal of fear in dangerous places including battlefields and lonely areas
Invokes the blessing of all siddhis and spiritual accomplishments
Especially effective when recited during Kalashtami or at midnight
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Bhairava, Bhairav Kavacham (भैरव कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Rooted in Rudrayamala Tantra, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow all siddhis and spiritual accomplishments and shield every part of the body from head to toe with Bhairav's protection. It is also said to remove fear in dangerous places including battlefields and lonely areas. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Kalashtami and Maha Shivaratri. With 9 verses and a reading time of about 10 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Bhairav Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Bhairav Kavacham and where does it come from?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhairav Kavacham (भैरव कवचम्) is a powerful nine-verse 'divine armour' (kavacha) hymn invoking Lord Bhairava — the fierce, protective form of Lord Shiva — to shield every limb of the body and every direction of space from danger, evil spirits, hostile forces and untimely death. The text is preserved in the Rudrayamala Tantra, one of the major Yamala-tantra scriptures of Shaiva-Shakta tradition. Its viniyoga declares: Brahma is the rishi, Gayatri is the chhanda, Shri Bhairava is the devata, Hrim is the bija, Klim is the shakti, Shrim is the kilaka, and the application is sarva-apad-vinashana and raksha — the destruction of all calamity and complete protection. (Sources: Rudrayamala Tantra; classical Bhairava upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Bhairava and what form is meditated upon in this kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Bhairava is the fierce ugra-svarupa of Lord Shiva, manifested to humble the pride of Lord Brahma and to act as the kshetrapala (protector of sacred ground) of every Shakti pitha and every Shaiva temple. The dhyana of this kavacham invokes Kalpanta-bhairavam — Bhairava as he appears at the end of an aeon — Nilanjana-samaprabham (radiant like dark collyrium), Trinetram (three-eyed), Dashahastam (ten-armed), Shula-khadga-dharam (bearing trident and sword), Naga-yajnopavitam (with serpent as sacred thread), Naga-kundala-bhushitam (adorned with serpent earrings), with the Devi seated on his left thigh — the form that destroys the fear of death itself (kala-bhayapaham). (Sources: Bhairav Kavacham dhyana shloka; Rudrayamala tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Which body parts does the Bhairav Kavacham protect?
seoFaqs.answer: The kavacham follows the classical anga-nyasa pattern, assigning a specific Bhairava-rupa to guard every part of the body from head to toe. Bhairava himself protects the head; Vatuka guards the forehead; Bhadra the eyebrows; Rudra the eyes; Krodha the ears; Ghrana-bhairava the nose; Maheshvara the throat and tongue; Kapalabhrit the chin and neck; Shuli the shoulders; Dandapani the arms; Khadgi the hands; Shankara the fingers; Raudra the nails; Vamana the heart; Kala the chest; Ganadhipa the navel; Bhishana the thighs; Rakta the knees; Bhutapa the calves; and finally Bhairava himself the feet. The closing verse seals all directions — east, south, west, north and the intermediates — plus zenith and nadir, with different Bhairava-murtis. (Sources: Bhairav Kavacham anga-nyasa.)

seoFaqs.question: What protections does the Bhairav Kavacham specifically promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Bhairav Kavacham makes some of the most explicit protection guarantees in the Tantric corpus. Sarva-apad-vinirmuktah — complete release from every calamity; sarva-siddhim avapnuyat — attainment of all siddhis. The text then names the most fearsome circumstances and promises immunity in each: rajadvare (in the king's court / before authority and law), shmashane cha (in the cremation ground), sangrame (on the battlefield), shatru-sankate (when surrounded by enemies), and bhuta-preta-pishachebhyo na bhayam vidyate kvachit (no fear whatsoever from ghosts, departed spirits or pishachas). It is therefore traditionally recited by tantriks, kshatriya warriors, and any devotee facing danger or supernatural disturbance. (Sources: Bhairav Kavacham Phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Bhairav Kavacham be recited for maximum power?
seoFaqs.answer: The Bhairav Kavacham is most powerful when recited on Tuesdays (Mangalvar) and Saturdays (Shanivar) — Bhairava's special days — and especially on every Kalashtami (Krishna Paksha Ashtami of every month, the monthly birth-day of Kala Bhairava), at midnight (nishita-kala) which is Bhairava's hora. Its most supreme parayana is on Maha Kalashtami in Margashirsha (Bhairava Jayanti). Other auspicious occasions include Maha Shivaratri, every Pradosh, the entire month of Margashirsha, and during the kshetrapala puja at Shakti pithas. The recitation should be done after bath, facing south, with a black-til (sesame) lamp, after first chanting the viniyoga and dhyana shlokas exactly as printed. (Sources: Kala Bhairava upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of regular Bhairav Kavacham recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Tantric acharyas attribute extensive benefits to sincere daily Bhairav Kavacham recitation: complete shielding of every limb of the body from accident, illness and assault; immunity from black magic (abhichara), occult attacks, evil-eye and curses; freedom from all bhuta-preta-pishacha-baadha (possession by departed spirits); protection in lonely places, forests, cremation grounds and at night; confidence in the king's court and victory in legal disputes; courage and victory on the battlefield and against enemies; removal of debt-fear, enemy-fear and untimely-death-fear (akala-mrityu-haram); attainment of the eight siddhis through the grace of the kshetrapala; and the supreme phala of fearless Shiva-bhakti. Many tantriks recite it 108 times on Kalashtami for sankalpa-siddhi. (Sources: Bhairava Tantra tradition; Rudrayamala phalashruti.)
